I bring eight business owners up here and I get a whiteboard and a marker and I say Everybody today hits the lottery And you each have 50 million dollars What are the first five things you're going to do? And we play the game and I write it on the whiteboard guess what's on everybody's top five list Yeah, walk away from their business every single one of them So who the shit's kidding who? All right Now we got to the real answer which their answer is the same as the guy about his job The real answer is they get up in the morning go to their business because they got to pay the bills That's their number one answer now if that's true Then their interest in their business is negatively motivated not positively motivated it is Obligation not desire linked and so in reality how excited about Improving it are they really? ready to be In the sales letter for this event I quoted Marlon Brando at one time said if somebody will just drop off and I forget the number now a Million dollars or five million out whatever it was on the island on the first day of every year. I'll never act again Now seriously what are your odds of selling him anything about? improving acting Not good right Like so I did the Success tour the Peter Low events for nine consecutive years 25 to 27 times a year's factory work because you did the same presentation Say it's our But still a little nuances, you know you can improve I Stopped thinking at all about improving that With about three and a half years left to go. I Had lost all interest I Didn't want to have a conversation about it Anybody that broached a conversation about it. I changed the topic. I Wasn't buying anything about how to improve and you couldn't have sold me anything about how to improve All right, I was showing up Essentially because I was obligated to do so by contract and for the money But I had no more interest in it That I have in like putting you guys all on a cruise ship for a week and sailing together Okay, the two things are right about at the same level, you know I mean you will never all my events are on land where there's a door I can leave You know, I can't swim so cruises with customers are out So I mean I was turned off. I mean I was doing my job And I was doing my job well, but I was doing it entirely out of obligation and so if Brian Tracy wanted to sit around a brainstorm how we could be more effective on the platform I wanted to go talk to Troy Aikman about What it was like to play for the Dallas Cowboys or I wanted to get the hell out of the room But I did not want to have that conversation So if that's where somebody is Here's what you got to get there at a starting point way the hell over there You're starting way over here and you've done left them behind So obligation Is not a highly motivational factor need so What does need look like when people do things out of need need looks like this We got to have it Now you don't got to have it their entire Nations that don't have it We are over in some of them trying to do nation building Okay, and they did as Clinton said about Afghanistan You know if we if we drop the big one on them, we will bomb them into the 20th century And so there are people what who don't use this they don't have it they don't know what it is My guess would be I have no idea my guess would be the president's brother who lives in the hut in Kenya Probably no probably not But we all consider it to be kind of a necessity If you went up into your hotel room tonight, and there was none You would be on the phone, right? And depending on when you discovered its absence You would you would be polite or you would be extremely aggravated But you would you would like consider this to be sort of Not an option in a hotel room. It's not like in the upgrade package only on the concierge floor Has toilet paper so you would be disappointed to discover that fact right however Having said all of that that we all agree we need it And we are unhappy being in any place for any length of time that doesn't have it if we have oatmeal for breakfast We're really unhappy being in any length of time if we're of a certain age and have oatmeal for breakfast We're really unhappy of being in any place that does not have this present having said all of that Is there anybody who is wildly excited about going to the store to buy it? and so Alley You're going to go to the store and buy toilet paper today, or you're going to go buy shoes, which is more interesting to you Shoes right I mean, you know we can have like a four-hour conversation about shoes We are not going to have a four-hour conversation about toilet paper So we need it, but nobody is really interested in it They try in TV commercials to get us interested in ply and But so we're not really interested in it We're not motivated by going to get it We're probably not going to read a direct mail piece about it We've done infomercials about some pretty boring stuff, but nobody's attempted a 28 and a half minute show about toilet paper Okay, it would be funny the testimonials would be fabulous But I mean I mean it would be funny, you know, but nobody's attempted it and nobody has any sort of Emotional payoff when they go buy it
